source.title: Hippocampal theta sequences reflect current goals.

confidence_rationale: Direct evidence that hippocampal theta sequences modulate their look-ahead extent based on goal distance and on whether the animal is mid-journey vs at-goal. The on-arrival contraction of look-ahead is the strongest piece for MECH-057b's framing: when the goal is reached, candidate generation stops extending forward, consistent with a completion-detection mechanism gating further candidacy. Mapping fidelity is moderate -- the paper measures look-ahead extent, not explicit candidacy suppression -- but the convergence with Pfeiffer & Foster gives mutual reinforcement.
failure_signatures: If theta sequence look-ahead were invariant to goal distance, the candidacy-gate framing (proposals tuned to complete on the current goal) would be weakened.
If on-arrival look-ahead remained extended in the same way as during journeys, the gate would not appear to detect completion.

mapping.source_claim_statement: Hippocampal theta sequences project ahead of the animal's current location to an extent that depends on the rat's current goal: look-ahead extends farther on journeys to distant goals than to proximal ones, and contracts on arrival at goals.
mapping.ree_translation: Hippocampal trajectory candidates are not generic forward-replay; their reach is matched to the active goal. In REE this is consistent with a candidacy gate that selects only completion-relevant trajectories. On goal arrival, look-ahead contracts -- a behavioural signature of the completion check firing and short-circuiting further propagation.
mapping.mapping_caveat: The paper does not measure suppression directly; it measures the distribution of look-ahead extents. The completion-detection inference rests on the on-arrival contraction, which has multiple alternative explanations (e.g. simple value gating without a candidacy mechanism).
mapping.source_context: Awake rats on a value-guided spatial decision task; CA1 ensemble; theta-cycle decoding.

confidence_components.notes: Strong source. Mapping discount because look-ahead modulation is consistent with several gating architectures, not uniquely with completion-verification.
